title: Teacher — My Class & Subjects
content:
Overview


The My Class page is where teachers create and manage their subjects. Each subject represents a class they teach, and students enroll in these subjects to receive assignments and grades. The page includes term and year filters to help teachers organize subjects across different academic periods.



Creating a Subject


  1. Click Create Subject at the top of the My Class page
  2. Enter a Subject Name — include the term and year for easy identification (e.g., "Biology (Spring 2026)" or "Calculus Fall 2026")
  3. Click Create Subject

A suggested naming format is shown as a tip: including the season and year helps differentiate subjects across terms. For example, a teacher who teaches Algebra 2 every semester should name them "Algebra 2 (Fall 2026)" and "Algebra 2 (Spring 2027)" rather than just "Algebra 2."


Once created, the subject appears in the subjects grid on the My Class page. Students can then find it through the Enrolment page and request to join.



Managing Subjects


The subjects grid shows all created subjects with filtering options:


  • Term filter: Dropdown to filter by term (Fall, Spring, Winter, Summer, September Term, January Term, May Term, or All Terms)
  • Year selector: Plus/minus buttons to change the academic year
  • Search: Filter subjects by name

Each subject card shows:


  • Subject name
  • Student count: Number of enrolled students
  • Actions: Options to manage the subject

Clicking a subject card opens the subject detail view where the teacher can manage students, view enrolments, and access the gradebook for that subject.



Adding Students to a Subject


There are two ways students get added to a subject:


  1. Student requests enrolment: Students browse available subjects through the Enrolment page in their Student Portal and submit a request. The teacher approves or rejects it from the Enrolment page in the Teacher Portal.
  2. Teacher manually adds students: From the subject detail view, click Manage Students to open the student search modal. Teachers can search for any student in the school by name or email and add them directly to the subject.

The student search modal also shows currently enrolled students in a dropdown for quick reference.



Term and Year Organization


The term and year filters at the top of the My Class page help teachers keep subjects organized across semesters. Teachers who teach multiple terms should:


  • Create a new subject for each term (even if it's the same course)
  • Include the term and year in the subject name
  • Use the filters to quickly find subjects from a specific term or year

This prevents overlap between different cohorts of students and keeps gradebooks separate for each term.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Name your subjects with the term and year — like 'Physics (Fall 2026)' — so you can tell them apart later."
  • "Students find your subjects through the Enrolment page. You'll see their requests in your Enrolment tab."
  • "You can also add students manually by searching for them in the Manage Students modal."
  • "Create a new subject for each term, even if it's the same course. That keeps your gradebooks separate."
  • "Use the term and year filters at the top to quickly find subjects from a specific semester."
